data.title: Stock market stability tested by stalled major indexes despite AI hype, indicating saturation in investors' sentiments.
data.description: In recent months, the main stock market indicators have largely remained stable, with investors becoming less enthusiastic about artificial intelligence.

data.title: A.I. efficiency may lead to more rate cuts, but Fed chair Warsh faces challenge convincing colleagues due to increased productivity potential.
data.description: Kevin M. Warsh, the potential Federal Reserve chair, could have a difficult time convincing his fellow members to reduce interest rates due to the possibility of increased productivity.

data.title: US tourism faces ongoing decline as the only major destination with fewer international visitors last year. Border scrutiny, ICE violence, and policy uncertainty contribute to the slump.
data.description: In the previous year, the United States experienced a decrease in international visitors, marking it as the sole major travel destination with a decline. This drop was influenced by heightened border security measures, instances of violence by ICE, and policies that were deemed erratic. Unfortunately, the trend does not appear to be improving in the new year.

data.title: Apollo Global Management injects $1B into Aldar Properties, marking their fifth investment since 2022, totaling $2.9B. Aldar aims to enhance balance sheet and fuel growth, a significant foreign investment in Abu Dhabi's private sector.
data.description: Apollo Global Management has announced a $1 billion investment in subordinated hybrid notes issued by Aldar Properties, a UAE-based real estate developer. This is the fifth investment in the company since 2022, bringing total commitments to about $2.9 billion. The investment aims to bolster Aldar's balance sheet flexibility and support its growth agenda, including landbank replenishment and expansions. This transaction is one of the largest foreign direct investments in Abu Dhabi's private sector. Aldar's management views this as enhancing its financial strategy and growth trajectory. Faisal Falaknaz, Aldar's Group Chief Financial and Sustainability Officer, highlighted that the issuance provides long-term, flexible capital to increase balance sheet resilience and capitalize on market opportunities. Aldar's plans focus on elevating share of stable, recurring income from its diversified portfolio. This investment solidifies Apollo's role as a capital partner for global companies.

data.title: JD.com to report Q4 and full year 2025 financial results on March 5, 2026.NASDAQ: JD, HKEX: 9618. Released before market opens.
data.description: On February 20, 2026, JD.com, Inc. stated its intention to unveil the unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2025 on March 5, 2026, prior to the U.S. market opening. As a prominent supply chain-focused technology and service firm, JD.com, Inc. operates under the tickers JD on NASDAQ and 9618 (HKD counter) and 89618 (RMB counter) on HKEX.

data.title: Phemex integrates Ondo Finance tokenized equities for RWA revolution, serving 10 million users worldwide.
data.description: In APIA, Samoa on February 20, 2026, Phemex, a crypto exchange focused on user experience, disclosed its incorporation of the entire range of Ondo Finance tokenized equities. This move places the exchange as a leader in the Real-World Asset (RWA) innovation, providing services to its extensive base of 10 million users worldwide.

data.title: Cyclist Tadej Pogačar joins KuCoin EU launch event in Vienna. Marks his first public appearance since partnership announcement.
data.description: In Vienna, Austria on February 20, 2026, celebrated cyclist Tadej Pogačar made a notable appearance alongside KuCoin executives during the official KuCoin EU launch event. This occasion signified Pogačar's initial public presence related to the platform's new collaboration. The event was attended by a significant number of individuals and marked the beginning of an exciting partnership.

data.title: GownCard launches collaborative platform in surgical teams, partners with leading health systems for pilot programs.
data.description: In Nashville, Tennessee on February 20, 2026, GownCard unveiled its official launch as a collaborative knowledge platform designed for surgical teams. The platform has initiated strategic pilot partnerships with prominent health systems. By teaming up with frontline clinicians, GownCard aims to centralize information and improve communication in surgical settings.

data.title: 2025 ezW2Correction software by Halfpricesoft.com aids CPAs with W-2 and W-3 corrections, enabling efficient processing.
data.description: A newly launched software, ezW2Correction, aims to assist tax professionals in minimizing errors, meeting IRS deadlines, and managing an unlimited number of correction forms effectively. Developed by Halfpricesoft.com and released on February 20, 2026, in REDMOND, Wash., this affordable tool provides step-by-step guidance to ensure accurate tax filings. It equips professionals with the confidence to handle corrections seamlessly, offering a user-friendly approach for enhanced productivity.

data.title: Trading Academy in Irvine announces a 4-day in-person Cryptocurrency & Digital Assets Immersion class to teach about the global financial system reshaped by digital assets.
data.description: In Irvine, California, on February 20, 2026, Trading Academy announced the expansion of its educational offerings to include a new four-day in-person Digital Assets & Cryptocurrency Immersion program. This initiative is in response to the ongoing transformation of the global financial landscape by digital assets and cryptocurrency. The program aims to provide individuals with a comprehensive understanding of these emerging technologies through hands-on learning experiences.

data.title: Sectra invites investors to presentation of 9-month report for 2025/2026 with President and CFO in Sweden on Feb. 20, 2026.
data.description: A Swedish company specializing in medical imaging IT and cybersecurity, Sectra (STO: SECT B), based in Linköping, has announced its invitation to investors, analysts, and media for a presentation regarding its nine-month report. The presentation will feature company President Torbjörn Kronander and CFO Jessica Holmquist.

data.title: US stock futures up despite Thursday's drop, awaiting Q4 GDP data release. 10-year Treasury bond yields 4.07%. Indices up: Dow Jones 0.26%, S&P 500 0.37%, Nasdaq 100 0.47%, Russell 2000 0.34%. SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust and Invesco QQQ Trust ETF also rose in premarket. Dropbox fell 4.16%, Copart dropped 6.64% post earnings reports. Fed likely to keep rates unchanged.
data.description: U.S. stock futures were up on Friday following a dip on Thursday, with all major benchmark indices showing positive trends. Investors await the fourth-quarter GDP data and the Personal Consumption Expenditure Price Index release. President Trump's ultimatum to Iran regarding its nuclear deal saw Tehran threatening retaliation against U.S. bases. The 10-year Treasury bond yield was at 4.07%, and the two-year bond at 3.47%. The FedWatch tool predicts a 94% chance of unchanged interest rates in March. Stock indices performed: Dow Jones +0.26%, S&P 500 +0.37%, Nasdaq 100 +0.47%, Russell 2000 +0.34%. SPY and QQQ ETFs were up premarket. Dropbox fell 4.16% despite strong Q4 earnings, predicting FY2026 sales slightly below estimates. Copart dropped 6.64% after disappointing Q2 earnings. Full article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: CSG group's Excalibur Intl. to supply tens of thousands of artillery/mortar ammo to NATO country in Western Europe in low hundreds of millions euros.
data.description: Excalibur International, a company under the CSG group, has secured a deal with the Ministry of Defense of a NATO member country in Western Europe. The contract entails the provision of tens of thousands of artillery and mortar ammunition, amounting to a total value in the low hundreds of millions of euros. The agreement was granted through a fast-tracked tender process, responding to the critical requirement of restocking the ammunition inventory for the armed forces of the NATO member country.

data.title: Metaplanet CEO denies allegations of concealing Bitcoin trades and losses amid growing investor backlash.
data.description: Simon Gerovich, from Metaplanet, responded to allegations of concealing losses and important information regarding their Bitcoin investments amidst growing investor discontent over leveraged Bitcoin holdings. The company has come under fire for not disclosing key details, with the backlash intensifying due to the increasing number of leveraged Bitcoin treasuries.
